input_dict.question: What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?
input_dict.source_summary: Kansas Wesleyan University's head football coaches with recorded win–loss totals in the provided passages include Coach Miller (0–1), W. F. Ragle (5–4), C. L. Williams (9–5), and Ron Dupree (11–18). Matt Drinkall was hired as Kansas Wesleyan's head coach in January 2014 (his win–loss record is not given in the supplied passages).
input_dict.target_summary:
Integrated evidence summary: Kansas Wesleyan University is a private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, in 1886. Available passages record multiple historical head football coaches and their win–loss totals: 
- Coach Miller — second head football coach, served one season (1906), compiling a record of 0–1. 
- W. F. Ragle — fifth head football coach, served one season (1915), compiling a record of 5–4. 
- C. L. Williams — third head football coach, served two seasons (1908–1909), compiling a record of 9–5. 
- Matt Drinkall — hired as head coach in January 2014 (no win–loss record provided in the supplied passages). 
Ambiguity: the phrase "the head football coach" could refer to any of these individuals; the win–loss record for the coach hired in 2014 (Matt Drinkall) is not present in the supplied evidence.
